observations.recommendation: 1.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Cash in Bank account.
2.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the proper disposition of dormant accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2023-008, for the cleansing of all dormant accounts.

3.5 We recommended and management agreed to require all personnel granted cash advances to liquidate the same promptly, especially at the end of the year, and refund immediately unexpended balances to comply with paragraph 5.8 of COA Circular 97-002; and for the proper recording of all expenses for the year.

4.6 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed to require:
4.6.1 The accounting unit and other offices concerned to verify each item of inventory and, thereafter, reconcile their records/reports regularly in order to determine the actual existing and recorded inventory of the municipality; and
4.6.2 The Inventory Committee to conduct semi-annual physical count of all inventoriable supplies/goods through the RPCI to come up with actual supplies on hand at period end.

5.11 We recommended that management:
5.11.1 Revisit its policy on repairs of motor vehicles with due consideration of COA allowable limit of repairs under COA Circular Nos. 85-55A and 2012-003;
5.11.2 Require the Property Division to prepare a comprehensive list and monitoring report of vehicles, including repairs and maintenance thereof; and
5.11.3 Consider disposing outmoded vehicles that have undergone recurring repairs which are not cost beneficial to the LGU.

6.12 We recommended that management:
6.12.1 Refrain from hiring COS/JO workers whose assigned tasks are similar with those of regular/permanent employees;
6.12.2 Perform an extensive assessment and evaluation of the LGU’s existing manpower and develop an effective and economical staffing plan to ascertain the necessity of hiring COS/JOs and prevent the overlapping of functions and the hiring of unnecessary job orders; and
6.12.3 Limit the hiring of COS workers in compliance with the provisions of COA-DBM JC No. 2, s.2020.

that the Municipal

and eliminate the
same in the ensuing
Consolidated
Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the intra -agency reciprocal accounts and eliminate the same in the ensuing Consolidated Financial Statements.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and Management agreed to require:
The Municipal Accountant and the Supply/Property Officer to verify each item of PPE and thereafter, reconcile their records/reports to come up with the actual existing and recorded PPE of the municipality;
The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books to correct the affected accounts in the financial statements; and
All offices concerned to report/return destroyed/unserviceable property that are no longer needed to the Supply Office for proper disposal, cancellation of PARs and preparation of required reports.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to record the loss of property and to set up the receivable account due from the Accountable Officer following the proposed adjusting entry, and thereafter demand payment from the AO; and
In all succeeding cases, notify immediately the Office of the Auditor for any loss of breeding stocks/livestock and file a request for relief from accountability within thirty (30) days from loss.

Draw the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the total amount transferred to STF under “TL-DRRM” in the TF books.

We recommended that management:
5.9.1	Require the accounting unit and other offices concerned to verify each item of inventory and, thereafter, reconcile their records/reports regularly in order to determine the actual existing and recorded inventory of the municipality; and

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed:
a. to comply with the DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-
3; DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2018-61; and COA Circular No.
94-013 on the recording of fund transfers from national government
agencies; and
b. to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the overstated
Subsidy Income, and understated Road networks and Government
Equity accounts as of December 31, 2018.

We have recommended Management, thru the Accounting and
Engineering Office, to prepare the PPE Local Road Network Ledger and
Property Cards and reconcile the balances with the Registry of Public
Infrastructure – Road Networks and Report on the Local Road Network.

We have recommended the Municipality to comply with COA Circular
No. 2015-008. Specifically, we have recommended the Municipal Mayor
to:
a. create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of
the existing local road network to facilitate the preparation of the
RPCLRN;
b. require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant
with the complete description and cost segregation of road components
for road projects; and,
c. require the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose in the Note to FS the
total road network of the Municipality.

We have reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor:
a. issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance (CFA) after one (1) year from
issuance date of Certificate of Completion (CoC); and,
b. impose on contractors, the appropriate Warranty Security with validity
periods covering one (1) year from date of CFA.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation system that will evaluate
if applied gender interventions responded to the gender issues
identified; and,
b. require the Local Sanggunian to update the existing LGU GAD Code
with the assistance of the GFPS so as to cater to the gender issues of the
present time.

We have recommended Management to issue demand letters to those who
were separated from the LGU service, and continue efforts to collect
unsettled disallowances.

We have recommended that
Management:
c. complete the relevant
supporting documents in filing
the request for authority to
write-off dormant fund transfers
as prescribed in Sections 7 and 8
of COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016; and,
d. file the request again thru the
COA Audit Team Leader (ATL)
and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) for verification and
validation.
observations.managementAction: Management coordinated with the focal person, barangay and the high school for gathering and reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: Management has yet to complete the supporting documents for submission.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
Management enjoin the concerned
department head’s compliance with
the requirements of Items VI and
VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008
and catch up with the transitory
provision in Item IX of the same
circular, specifically:
a. Thru the GSO, to maintain a
Local Road Inventory and Road
Map and keep a complete Local
Road Network Property Card for
all roads and its components; 
b. Thru the Municipal Accountant,
to disclose in the Notes to
Financial Statements the total
road network system of the
recorded in the agency; keep and
maintain books subsidiary records
for roads and road component for every road
network and prepare a lapsing
schedule for the computation of the
depreciation for each useful lives of
component at the end of the
year; and,
c. Thru the Municipal Engineer, to
provide the local accountant and
the general services officer with
complete description and cost
segregation of road components
for road projects.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineering Office submitted reports of the Local Road Network and its physical count.
The Municipal Accountant recorded in the books the total road network disclosed in the Notes.
The Municipal Engineer provided description of local road as shown in the Report of Local Road Network.
observations.reason: No depreciation was recognized in the books because Management did not estimate the useful lives of the local road network.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
Management:
c. strengthen its monitoring and
inspection mechanism, and
proceed with the issuance of the
Certificate of Completion after
thorough evaluation of the project
as required; 
d. issue the Certificate of Final
Acceptance after one (1) year
from date of CoC to cover the
Defects Liability Period; and
e. impose on contractors, PS with
validity periods covering
implementation phase up to final
acceptance and WS with validity
periods covering one year from
the CFA date.
observations.managementAction:
Management is exerting efforts in the implementation and monitoring of infrastructure projects.
Management required the posting of warranty securities by the contractors.
Management is gradually opening/creating plantilla positions for job order personnel.
observations.reason: Management issued the CoC and CFA at the same date for some projects; warranty securities were required from contractors but certificates of final acceptance were not consistently issued one year after CoC.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management submit copies of the perfected contracts including the required supporting documents within five days upon perfection/issuance, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) transmitted 11 perfected contracts under the Development Fund and LDRRMF for CY 2018; 45% were submitted on time. Perfected contracts under the Trust Funds were not submitted.
observations.reason: Some perfected contracts were submitted, but not all (Trust Fund contracts not submitted); only 45% were submitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
Management submit the required
supporting documents for the grant
of RATA of Sangguniang Bayan
members together with the monthly
payroll on a timely basis.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan Secretary submitted the required supporting documents.
observations.reason: The attendance Sheets, Minutes of the Meetings and Leave of Absences were attached to the payroll submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
Management, thru the HRMO-Designate, employ proper monitoring of leave credits and follow Sections 22 & 23 on the monetization of leave credits.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO-Designate regularly updated the leave ledger cards of employees and official.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management immediately cause the settlement of the long outstanding disallowances of former employees either by collecting directly from them, or by collecting from the other persons solidarily liable.
observations.managementAction: Management collected some outstanding disallowances of former employees.
observations.reason: Collected a total in CY 2018; the balance as of December 31, 2018 is P24,848.00.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
Management:
a. require the GAD Focal Point to administer the HGDG test on the LGU’s GAD PPAs in the preparation of Accomplishment Report, as basis for determining the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD Budget; and,
b. administer the HGDG test on its PPAs for CY 2018, the results of which shall be the basis in estimating the cost of gender mainstreaming and GAD Budget; and,
c. if and when necessary, conduct a training program on the sample exercises of the HGDG for the incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of and/administration of the HGDG.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS submitted the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) for CY 2018. A training with sample exercises on HGDG test was conducted.
observations.reason: HGDG test was administered on the CY 2018 PPAs but no evaluation was made on the actual expenditure attributed to GAD; the cost of gender mainstreaming was not identified.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that
Management strategize with the
Municipal Engineering Office and
other offices concerned to fast track
the implementation of projects to
immediately address the needs of
target beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Engineering Office exerted efforts in implementing projects despite right-of-way issues and PPAs realignments.
observations.reason: The rate of on-going or not implemented PPAs decreased to 18.42%.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management coordinate with the Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the SEF to meet the existing and growing demands of basic education services, of which the Fund intends to provide. Further, to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly update the LSB of availability of funds for appropriation.
observations.managementAction: The SEF was fully used for educational services.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management comply with the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003, and its implementing rules and regulations, including the establishment of a Solid Waste Management Disposal Facility.
observations.managementAction: Completed the consultation with the barangays and the assessment by the Environmental Management Bureau (EMB). The Municipality completed its 10-year SWM Plan which was approved by the National SWM Commission. Functional MRFs and segregation of wastes at source were implemented at municipal and barangay level. Executive Order No. 58 s. 2017 and LSB Resolution No. 2018-314 on the establishment of solid waste management program of the Municipality was declared valid and operative.
observations.reason: Assessment by the Mines and Geosciences Bureau (MGB) is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider reducing the employment of Job Order personnel only for approved local projects authorized by the Sanggunian after evaluating all manpower complements whether or not there exist duplication of work or excessive employment of contract of service workers.
observations.managementAction: The number of plantilla positions for job order personnel was gradually opened/created. The number of positions for job order additional personnel for CY 2018 decreased to 71.
observations.reason: There was a gradual reduction in job order personnel but full rationalization not yet achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management cause the refund of the excess PEI granted to officials and employees in CY 2013 and 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant collected a total of P379,400.00 in CY 2018.
observations.reason: The uncollected balance as of December 31, 2018 is P36,800.00.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended Management to:
a) require the GAD Focal Point to administer the HGDG test on the LGU’s GAD PPAs in the preparation of Accomplishment Report, as basis for determining the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD Budget; 
b) administer the HGDG test on its PPAs for CY 2017, the results of which shall be the basis in estimating the cost of gender mainstreaming and GAD Budget; and 
c) if and when necessary, conduct a training program on the HGDG for the incoming year and seek assistance of a gender analysis expert in the use of and/administration of the HGDG.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: (Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 12 in Part II of this report).

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a. complete the relevant supporting documents in filing the request for authority
to write-off dormant fund transfers as prescribed in Sections 7 and 8 of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and,
b. file the request again thru the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or
Supervising Auditor (SA) for verification and validation.

3.6 We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Strengthen its monitoring and inspection mechanism, and proceed with the
issuance of the Certificate of Completion after thorough evaluation of the
project as required;
b. Issue the Certificate od Final Acceptance after one (1) year from date of CoC
to cover the Defects Liability Period; and
c. Impose on contractors, PS with validity periods covering implementation
phase up to final acceptance and WS with validity periods covering one year
from CFA date.

5.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to submit the required
supporting documents for the grant of RATA of Sangguniang Bayan members
together with the monthly payroll on a timely basis.

We recommended Management advice the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected. Further, require the submission by the recipients of liquidation reports as bases in the recording of adjustments to the Due from LGUs account.
observations.managementAction: Unutilized fund transfers were returned to the source agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Submit a monthly summary of leave applications of officials receiving RATA and the work schedules or performance standards of the Sanggunian for CY 2016; and report.
b) Support the grant of RATA for the ensuing year with the following documents:
b.1 For regular officials observing an 8-hour work day, copy of monthly Daily Time Record (DTR) with supporting Certificates of Appearances and Leave Applications; and
b.2 For the members of its Sangguniang Bayan, minutes of sessions and/or monthly work schedule attendance and leave applications or appearance if applicable.
observations.managementAction: Daily Time Records (DTRs) with Leave Applications were submitted. Attendance and Minutes of sessions with leave applications submitted were only until July 2017.
observations.reason: (Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 7 in Part II of this report).

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the variance between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the General Fund and Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The balances of the accounts were reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management submit copies of perfected contracts including the supporting documents within five days after perfection/issuance, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Perfected contracts were submitted by the BAC Secretariat. Four (4) contracts were submitted but supporting documents were lacking.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management to justify the fund transfers made not in accordance with the provisions of the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations and submit additional documents to supplement the justifications.
observations.managementAction: Lacking supporting documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management that for the ensuing year, they will submit within the prescribed period the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Monthly LDRRMF reports were submitted by the LDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management refrain from charging ineligible expenses from the LDRRMF and utilize the DRRM fund for disaster activities and projects pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and NDRRMC/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: Only eligible expenses were charged to the fund for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management refrain from charging ineligible expenses from the LDRRMF and utilize the DRRM fund for disaster activities and projects pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and NDRRMC/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: Efforts were made to comply with the provision of R.A. No. 9003. The Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and landfill is still on process. Quarterly clean-up drives.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended Management to require the offices concerned to execute all PPAs planned for implementation during the year to immediately address the needs and aspirations of the beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Most of the planned projects and programs for CY 2017 were implemented.
observations.reason: (Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 9 in Part II of this report).

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require all elected officials and heads of offices to undergo capacity-building programs on GAD. For economy, invite competent speakers and conduct in-house training to maximize the participation of the focal point system, elected officials, heads of offices, employees and other stakeholders, i.e. Barangay officials, to harmonize GAD-related activities in the ensuing year. Such capacity building programs on GAD shall include, but not limited to: gender sensitivity training, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework, Gender Responsive LGU Ka Ba Self-Assessment Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines and others.
observations.managementAction: Development Programs (GAD Module 1) and trainings for its employees and officials were conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated records, identify and settle all forwarded balances, and conduct periodic reconciliation with the General Services Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant updated the PPE records and identified the forwarded balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider reducing the employment of Job Order personnel only for approved local projects authorized by the Sanggunian after evaluating all manpower complements whether or not there exist duplication of work or excessive employment of contract of service workers.
observations.managementAction: Plantilla positions were created to accommodate job order employees. Total number of job order employees for CY 2017 decreased to 83.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the NGO/PO to submit the final Fund Utilization Report to support the completion of the project. Otherwise, the Municipal Accountant will reclassify the account or request for write-off of account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted request for write-off. (Restated under Audit Observation No. 1 in Part II of this report).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management cause the refund of the excess PEI granted to officials and employees in CY 2013 and 2014.
observations.managementAction: A total of P290,800.00 was collected as of December 31, 2017. The Municipality deducted payments twice for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that Management advice the Municipal Accountant to
adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 and prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts
affected. Further, require the submission by the recipients of liquidation
reports as bases in the recording of adjustments to the Due from LGUs
account.

2.6 We recommended and Management agreed to:

c) Submit a monthly summary of leave applications of officials
receiving RATA and the work schedules or performance
standards of the Sanggunian for CY 2016; and

d) Support the grant of RATA for the ensuing year with the
following documents:

b.1 For regular officials observing an 8-hour work day, copy of
monthly Daily Time Record (DTR) with supporting
Certificates of Appearances and Leave Applications;

b.2 For the members of the Sangguniang Bayan, minutes of
sessions and/or monthly work schedule attendance, and leave
applications or certificates of appearance, if applicable.

3.5 We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management
instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the variance between the
Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the General Fund and
Trust Fund.

4.5 We recommended and Management agreed to submit copies of perfected
contracts including the supporting documents within five days after
perfection/issuance, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

5.7 We recommended Management to justify the fund transfers made not in
accordance with the provisions of the aforementioned laws, rules and
regulations and submit additional documents to supplement the
justifications.

6.4 We recommended and Management agreed that for the ensuing year,
they will submit within the prescribed period the monthly Report on the
Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to the Audit Team.

7.7 We recommended that Management refrain from charging ineligible
expenses from the LDRRMF and utilize the DRRM fund for disaster
activities and projects pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2012-73 and NDRRMC/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1.

8.7 We recommended that management formulate strategies to comply with
the mandates set forth in RA 9003, particularly on the establishment of a
Solid Waste Facility.

observations.managementAction:
Identified the forwarded balances.
Effected the proposed adjusting journal entry.

Submitted on time some of the reports to the audit team. Reports like the
Monthly
Utilization of
LDRRMF and the
RCPPE were not
submitted on
time. (See
Observation No.
6 on this report.)

Established a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books.
Identified balances of the prior years.
Maintained subsidiary ledgers for every transfer.
Necessary adjustments have been made in the books.

Management justified that to prevent the designation of regular officials and employees from performing other functions, job order employees were hired to supplement the manpower of the MLGU.
Also, due to PS limitation, the LGU is not capable to create Plantilla Positions were created.
Executed most of the planned PPAs for implementation during the year. implementation 78.18% were implemented during the year.
Management is employing efforts to comply. It has crafted ordinances on solid waste management, municipal appropriated funds and implemented projects on waste management and disposal.
observations.reason:
The municipality still failed to
establish a
disposal facility.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant justified that there is no need to establish a Special account and Subsidiary ledgers for 20% DF were being maintained as the fund is monitored properly.
Collected a total amount of ₱45,600.00 and ₱34,200.00 respectively as of December 31, 2016. The Municipality deducted payments on a piecemeal basis.
Adjustments on the LGUs contribution to P100.00 only for 2015, and onwards.
